title: Non-Record: BPB 1.1334 — 7000-Step Training + Mixed Int6/Int8 Quantization + Legal TTT
author: Christopher-Lee-McClendon

data.scope: int6 per-row for attention projections and MLP weights; int8 per-tensor for layer norms, value embeddings, biases, embedding tables

data.description: 11-layer architecture with ReLU² MLP, Cross-sequence attention (XSA), Bigram hash embeddings, Partial RoPE, U-Net skip connections, LN depth scaling, Value embeddings on layers 9-10, Late QAT
Late QAT enabled at step 6476 with GPTQ-lite clip search (5 candidates per row)

novel_contributions: Extended training to 7000 steps with longer warmdown cosine anneal for better convergence
Mixed int6/int8 quantization scheme combining per-row int6 QAT for large tensors and per-tensor int8 for sensitive smaller tensors to keep artifact under 16MB
GEPA architecture combining ReLU² MLP, Cross-sequence attention, Bigram hash embeddings, Partial RoPE, U-Net skip connections, LN depth scaling, and late QAT
Legal score-first test-time training (TTT) with SGD momentum for 10 epochs per chunk, freezing first 2 blocks, yielding a −0.0142 BPB improvement
Fewer TTT epochs (10 vs 30) with faster evaluation time (40% less wall time) while achieving better final BPB
